teamObjective.explanation: Partner has only 2 cards left and the hand is tagged partner_near_finish, so the goal is to let partner win or cheaply handle the lead and possibly go out.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner likely has a compact hand and may be close to going out, but the exact ranks and suits of their remaining cards are unknown.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents hold the majority of the unseen cards; their exact strength is unknown at the opening lead.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of diamonds keeps my stronger cards (4-5-6) for later, gives partner the best chance to win or take the lead cheaply, and avoids revealing high-card strength this early.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Slightly stronger but still weakens the ability to play a low card later and does not materially help partner.
Medium-high card is more likely to win the trick myself, leaving partner unable to lead and potentially stranded.
Uses my strongest non-trump card on an opening lead where opponents are likely to have higher cards or trumps, wasting a useful later card.

risks: Partner may not be able to beat a low 3 if their remaining cards are also low
An opponent may take the trick with a higher card or trump
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the lowest possible card I minimize resource expenditure and retain 4-5-6 for subsequent tricks or to regain the lead.

notes: The verifier supplied only single-card plays; among them, the lowest card aligns with partner-support strategy on an opening lead.
